topic: winding Fiona up about baby names

turns.content: my sister is trying to pick a name for her wee one and I have been winding her up about it all week. its the most fun ive had in months.
Ha, what kind of suggestions have you been making?
told her she should call the bairn hamish. then when she said no I suggested angus. then fergus. then magnus. basically just going through every name that sounds like a highland cow.
You're basically naming cattle at this point. I assume she's not taking your suggestions seriously?
she told me to stop texting her or shed block me. which she wont because she needs me to babysit. thats my leverage and she knows it.
Strategic babysitting leverage -- that's cold but effective. What kind of names is she actually considering?
normal ones. boring ones. she keeps going back and forth between about fifteen options and asking me what I think. and every time I just say hamish again. its a perfect system.
The commitment to the bit is impressive. Does she have a partner weighing in on the name, or is it all down to her?
aye her partner has opinions but fiona is fiona. she will decide and he will agree. thats how it works in our family. the women decide things and the men nod.
Sounds like a well-established family dynamic. Are you genuinely going to keep pushing Hamish, or do you actually have a real preference?

ngl I actually quite like the name ruairidh which she had on the list at one point. but I made the mistake of saying that so now she says its ruined because she pictures me saying it. apparently I say everything in a tone that makes it sound sarcastic.
To be fair, based on this conversation alone, I can see why she might think that. Ruairidh is a great name though. Maybe if you stop saying Hamish for a week she'll circle back to it.

aye but then id lose momentum on the hamish campaign and I canny have that. sent her a wee picture of a highland cow today with the caption 'uncle callum took hamish for a walk'. she left me on read for four hours.

Four hours of silence is actually a strong reaction -- you've clearly hit a nerve. The highland cow photo is a nice escalation though. Do you think she'll eventually just pick a name without telling you, to avoid the commentary?

prob. honestly whatever she picks ill be happy. im just enjoying winding her up because thats what brothers are for. the bairn could be called anything and id still be there every weekend making faces at it.
That's actually quite sweet, in a very roundabout way. Sounds like you're a good uncle even if you're a terrible naming consultant.
terrible naming consultant is going on my cv right under farm veterinarian. right im off to think of more cow names. cheers.
Good luck with the campaign. If Hamish doesn't work out, might I suggest Morag. Have a good one.
